verses.text: The Chronicler opens his history at a battlefield of defeat: Israel flees the Philistines on Gilboa, Saul's sons fall, and the king is wounded by archers. The reign that began with promise ends in disaster. A life that turns from God comes at last to ruin.

The Chronicler opens his history at a battlefield of defeat: Israel flees the Philistines on Gilboa, Saul's sons fall, and the king is wounded by archers. The reign that began with promise ends in disaster. A life that turns from God comes at last to ruin.

The Chronicler opens his history at a battlefield of defeat: Israel flees the Philistines on Gilboa, Saul's sons fall, and the king is wounded by archers. The reign that began with promise ends in disaster. A life that turns from God comes at last to ruin.

Wounded and dreading capture, Saul falls on his own sword, and his armour-bearer follows; so Saul and his house die together in a day. The collapse is total. No gifts or early anointing can save a man who abandons obedience to God.

Wounded and dreading capture, Saul falls on his own sword, and his armour-bearer follows; so Saul and his house die together in a day. The collapse is total. No gifts or early anointing can save a man who abandons obedience to God.

Wounded and dreading capture, Saul falls on his own sword, and his armour-bearer follows; so Saul and his house die together in a day. The collapse is total. No gifts or early anointing can save a man who abandons obedience to God.
Seeing the king dead, the men of Israel flee their cities, and the Philistines move in. One man's ruin scatters a whole people. The sin of a leader brings loss far beyond himself.
The Philistines strip Saul, behead him, and fasten his head in the temple of Dagon. The fallen king is made a trophy for an idol. He who would not honour God is dishonoured by God's enemies.
The Philistines strip Saul, behead him, and fasten his head in the temple of Dagon. The fallen king is made a trophy for an idol. He who would not honour God is dishonoured by God's enemies.
The Philistines strip Saul, behead him, and fasten his head in the temple of Dagon. The fallen king is made a trophy for an idol. He who would not honour God is dishonoured by God's enemies.

But the men of Jabesh-gilead recover the bodies and bury them, fasting seven days, repaying an old kindness. Even in the darkest hour, gratitude and courage shine. A flicker of loyal love honours the LORD's anointed even in disgrace.

But the men of Jabesh-gilead recover the bodies and bury them, fasting seven days, repaying an old kindness. Even in the darkest hour, gratitude and courage shine. A flicker of loyal love honours the LORD's anointed even in disgrace.

Saul died for his transgression, for not keeping God's word, for consulting a medium, and he 'enquired not of the LORD'; so God turned the kingdom to David. Here is the verdict on his life. To seek the LORD is the way of blessing; to forsake Him, the way of ruin.
